headline: AI hardware trades hitting a wall
detail: Optical and memory stocks are selling off as the AI hardware momentum trade stalls. This hits AVGO directly — it's a core AI networking chip name — and puts pressure on SMH broadly. For a margin account concentrated in chips, this is the single most important story today.

headline: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on Digital Services Tax countries
detail: Countries taxing American tech firms could face 100% tariffs, escalating trade tension around the sector. This creates multiple compression risk for high-multiple tech names like PLTR, which operates globally. Margin positions in growth tech are most vulnerable to sudden repricing on tariff headlines.

headline: Iran violated ceasefire, Hormuz drones confirmed
detail: Trump confirmed Iran drone attacks on Strait of Hormuz ships, raising geopolitical risk and oil prices. A sustained oil spike historically rotates institutional money out of tech and into energy, pressuring your entire book. Watch for a risk-off move that could accelerate selling in already-weak names like AVGO and PLTR.

headline: Meta AI push not lifting the stock
detail: Despite heavy AI investment, Meta isn't getting rewarded — a warning sign that AI narrative alone no longer drives tech prices higher. This matters because names like PLTR and CRWD are priced for growth perfection. If the market stops paying for AI hype, your most stretched valuations get hit hardest.
